Items.Title: LCH 是 UI 的最佳色彩空间 | LCH is the best color space for UI
Items.Description:
这使得 LCH 非常适合创建可访问、一致和可预测的颜色系统。 在这篇博文中，我们将探讨：

  • 为什么它比 HSL 更好

  • 它如何帮助您进行设计

什么是 LCH？

在过去的几十年里，我们一直在 Web 上使用 sRGB 色彩空间。 再加上 sRGB 是根据我们的显示器工作方式设计的，它不太适合创造出色的用户体验。 这就是 LCH 的用武之地。

从理论上讲，它可以描述无限量的颜色，但是，对我们来说，重要的是它的色域比 sRGB 的色域更大。 更多颜色意味着更漂亮的网站。

起初看起来类似于 HSL（色相、饱和度和亮度），但这些空间中的每一个都以不同的方式描述颜色。 让我们分别看各个部分。

亮度描述了颜色的相对亮度。 但是，每个空间的处理方式都不同。

  • 例如，要创建浅蓝色，您可以将白色添加到纯蓝色色调中。

  • 在下面的示例中，我们看到 HSL 和 LCH 中表示的蓝色和黄色的相同色调。 每个阴影都有最大的色度或饱和度，我们只是改变亮度值。

    请注意，HSL 颜色是如何以 50 的亮度最为鲜艳和多彩的。那是因为没有混合黑色或白色，它只是纯粹的色调。 这是描述颜色亮度的根本错误方式，因为在相同 HSL 亮度值的情况下，黄色看起来比蓝色亮得多。

    另一方面，在 LCH 中，黄色和蓝色的亮度都在逐渐增加，黄色的阴影看起来并不比蓝色的浅。

    比较色度和饱和度有点棘手，因为两者的工作方式不同。 但总的来说，这些值描述了颜色的“丰富”或“纯净”程度。

    • 在下面的示例中，我们在 LCH 和 HSL 中看到相同的蓝色色调，我们只是以相等的增量更改饱和度或色度值。 请注意 LCH 中的色调几乎融合在一起，而 HSL 中有明显的阶梯。

      在下一个示例中，我们将色调更改为浅蓝色，您可以再次看到 LCH 中的混合和 HSL 中的步骤。

      色调在两种颜色空间中的工作方式几乎相同。 色谱从红色开始，经过绿色、蓝色和红色。 唯一的区别是 LCH 中的色调与 HSL 相比略有偏移。

      请注意，在下面的示例中，HSL 中的色调值为 0 是纯红色，而对于 LCH，它是粉红色的红色。 该示例还说明了 LCH 的感知均匀性——所有颜色都具有相同的亮度和饱和度，唯一改变的是色调。 您可以看到在 LCH 中颜色开始混合，而在 HSL 中它们完全不同。

      为 UI 选择颜色的历史

      在 2000 年代，仅使用 HEX 或 RGB 为网站选择颜色是很常见的。 那时没有那么多花哨的颜色选择工具，HSL 也没有那么普遍。 例如，如果您需要橙色，则可以将红色添加一点绿色，然后就可以结束了。

      这种盲目的颜色选择方式很快被 Photoshop 及其颜色工具所取代。 多年后，我们仍然在 Figma 或 Sketch 中使用相同的工具。 如今，黄金标准是 HSL，因为它易于使用。

      最近很多阴影生成器和调色板生成器开始出现。 这些工具通常比仅仅改变给定色调的 HSL 亮度更智能。 但是，通常，您不会获得太多自定义。

      这就是 LCH 的用武之地。它是创建统一、可访问和可预测的 UI 调色板的最佳方式。 这需要一些时间，但我们相信设计师最终会因为 HSL 的不一致性而停止使用它，并改用 LCH 或一些类似的色彩空间。

      有关 LCH 好处的更多信息，请查看Stripe（支付平台）的这篇精彩文章，了解他们如何使调色板易于访问。

      开始使用 LCH 色彩空间的最佳方法是使用Atmos 。 我们创建 Atmos 的目的是为每个人提供LCH 色彩空间的好处，从爱好开发人员到专业设计师，旨在为他们的设计系统创建调色板。

      由于 LCH 色彩空间的特性，在 UI 设计方面，使用它可以更轻松地完成几件事。 这些都是：

      由于一致的对比度，内置的可访问性

      如果您曾经使用过 HSL，您可能会注意到具有相同亮度的两种不同颜色具有截然不同的对比度。 由于亮度值在 LCH 中的工作方式，具有相同亮度的不同颜色将具有几乎相同的对比度。 这使得确保可访问性变得更加容易。

      可互换的颜色

      由于色彩空间的感知均匀性，您可以轻松地交换颜色，因为它们知道它们将具有相同的对比度。 这使得在 UI 中尝试不同的颜色变得更加容易。

      灵活的调色板

      在 LCH 中为您的调色板添加颜色或阴影很简单。 无需记住您是如何创建调色板的。 如果您匹配其他颜色的亮度和饱和度级别，您可以确定新颜色将具有相同的对比度并且在感知上是均匀的。

      如果您还没有注意到我们喜欢 LCH 色彩空间。 在我们看来，它是目前创建 UI 调色板的最佳色彩空间。 综合考虑，LCH 有一个缺点，那就是流行的设计工具、网络或移动应用程序尚未广泛支持这一事实。 但是将颜色从 LCH 转换为 HEX（或 RGB）是所有好处的一个小折衷。

      适合所有人的 LCH 游乐场

      现在轮到你了！ 我们为任何有兴趣在 LCH 颜色空间中探索颜色和尝试 UI 调色板的人创建了Atmos 。 来吧，应用您的新知识，用Atmos创造一些很棒的东西。



原文:

LCH is a color space designed to model human perception of color which means it’s perceptually uniform. This makes LCH great for creating color systems that are accessible, consistent, and predictable. In this blogpost we will explore:

  • What LCH is all about

  • Why it’s better than HSL

  • How it can help you in design

What is LCH?

For the past few decades, we’ve been using sRGB color space on the Web. sRGB is limited to 16.7 million colors which might seem like a lot, but in the past few years our pc, laptop, and phone displays evolved fast and it’s not uncommon to have a display with more than 100% sRGB coverage. Coupled with the fact that sRGB was designed according to how our displays work, it’s not very well suited for creating great user experiences. That’s where LCH comes in.

LCH (or HCL, CIELAB, LAB, CIELUV, CIELCH) is a color space that was first defined in 1976 by International Commission on Illumination (CIE), hence the name CIELAB. CIE is the international authority on light, illumination, color, and color spaces. LCH’s main goal is to achieve perceptual uniformity, which means that it should behave according to how our eyes work. It can, in theory, describe an infinite amount of color, however, for us, what is important is that its color gamut is larger than the one from sRGB. More colors mean even more beautiful websites.

LCH stands for Lightness, Chroma, and Hue. Which at first looks similar to HSL (Hue, Saturation, and Lightness) but each of these spaces describes colors differently. Let’s look at the parts separately.

LCH Lightness vs HSL Lightness

Lightness describes the relative brightness of a color. However, each space goes about it differently.

  • HSL lightness resembles the amount of black or white added to a pure color. For example to create light blue you add white to a pure blue hue.

  • LCH lightness describes the relative brightness to a similarly illuminated white

In the example below we see the same hue of Blue and Yellow represented in HSL and LCH. Each shade has maximum chroma or saturation and we are only changing the lightness value.

Notice how the HSL colors are the most vibrant and colorful with the lightness of 50. That’s because there is no black or white mixed in, it’s just the pure hue. This is a fundamentally incorrect way of describing the lightness of color because the yellow looks much lighter than the blue with the same HSL lightness value.

On the other hand in LCH both yellow and blue are gradually increasing in lightness and the yellow shades don’t look any lighter than the blue ones.

LCH Chroma vs HSL Saturation

Comparing Chroma and Saturation is a bit tricky because both work differently. But in general, these values describe how “rich” or “pure” the color is.

  • HSL saturation has a fixed range of 0 (grey) to 100 (purest)

  • LCH chroma has a range of 0 to approximately 131. Not all colors reach this number because only some colors can be so vibrant on our screens

In the example below we see the same hue of Blue in LCH and HSL we are only changing the Saturation or Chroma value in equal increments. Notice how the shades in LCH almost blend together while there are pronounced steps in HSL.

In this next example, we changed the hue to Light Blue and again you can see the blending in LCH and steps in HSL.

LCH hue vs HSL hue

Hue works almost identically in both color spaces. The color spectrum starts at red and goes through green, blue, and back to red. The only difference is that hues in LCH are slightly shifted when compared to HSL.

Notice how in the example below hue value of 0 in HSL is pure red and for LCH it’s a pink-ish red. The example also illustrates the perceptual uniformity of LCH — all colors have the same lightness and saturation, the only thing that changes is the hue. You can see that in LCH the colors start to blend, while in HSL they are wildly different.

History of choosing colors for UI

In the 2000’s it was very common to choose colors for a website solely with HEX or RGB. At that time there weren’t that many fancy tools for picking colors and HSL weren’t that widespread. If you needed an orange color, for example, you would take a red color add a bit of green and call it a day.

This blind way of picking colors was very quickly replaced with Photoshop and its color tools. Years later and we still use the same tools in Figma or Sketch. Nowadays the gold standard is HSL because of its ease of use.

Recently a lot of shade generators and palette generators started popping up. These tools are usually smarter than just changing HSL Lightness across the given shades. However, usually, you don’t get much customization.

This is where LCH comes in. It is the best way to create uniform, accessible, and predictable UI color palettes. It will take some time, but we’re confident designers will eventually stop using HSL because of its inconsistencies and switch over to LCH or some similar color space.

For more information on the benefits of LCH check out this great post by Stripe (the payment platform) on how they made their color palette accessible.

The best way to start playing around with LCH color space is to use Atmos. We created Atmos to offer the benefits of LCH color space to everyone from hobby developers to professional designers aimed at creating a color palette for their design system.

LCH makes UI design a breeze

Thanks to the properties of LCH color space several things are so much easier to do with it when it comes to UI design. These are:

Built-in accessibility thanks to consistent contrast

If you’ve ever worked with HSL you probably noticed that two different colors with the same lightness have wildly different contrast ratios. Because of how the lightness values work in LCH different colors with the same lightness will have almost identical contrast ratios. This makes ensuring accessibility so much easier.

Interchangeable colors

Because of the perceptual uniformity of the color space, you can easily swap colors knowing that they’ll have the same contrast ratio. This makes experimenting with different colors in UI so much easier.

Flexible color palette

Adding colors or shades to your palette in LCH is simple. There is no need to remember how you’ve created the palette. If you match the lightness and saturation levels of other colors you can be sure that the new color will have the same contrast ratio and will be perceptually uniform.

HSL is dead — Long live LCH

If you didn’t notice already we LOVE the LCH color space. In our opinion, it is currently the best color space for creating UI color palettes. All things considered, LCH has one disadvantage and that is the fact that is not widely supported in popular design tools, on the web, or in mobile apps, yet. But converting colors from LCH to HEX (or RGB) is a small tradeoff for all the benefits.

LCH Playground for everyone

Now it’s your turn! We’ve created Atmos for anyone interested in exploring colors and experimenting with UI color palettes in LCH color space. Go ahead and apply your new knowledge and create something awesome with Atmos.

Items.Title: 如何从 Web2 过渡到 Web3 | How to Transition from Web2 to Web3
Items.Description:
你好呀！ 我的名字是 Dany，我是一名全栈 Web 开发人员，目前正在从 Web2 过渡到 Web3。 我的目标是全职从事 Web3 工作，并把我所有的时间都用于让 Web 更加去中心化。

我在这段旅程中已经有大约 4 个月的时间了，想和你分享我的故事和我的建议。 你可能听说过这个叫做 Web3 的东西，关于去中心化、加密、区块链和 NFT。 也许您还问自己“我应该进入 Web3”或“我如何才能进入 Web3”。

这些是我现在想在这里回答的问题。

重要的是要注意，本指南特别适用于已经在技术领域并希望过渡的人。 对于想开始从事技术工作的人，我将来会单独发一篇文章！

议程

  • 找个理由
  • 社区是关键
  • 学习基础知识
  • 资源
  • 构建项目

对我来说，这是最重要的一步。 如果你找到一个理由，你将永远不会失去动力。 在一个每天都在变化的空间里，你需要动力。 你上周学到的东西现在可能已经过时了。 如果你缺乏动力，这可能会让你崩溃。

我的动机是去中心化。 我的目标是完全去中心化我们的网络，原因很简单。 我希望互联网由我们、用户运营，而不是由大型科技公司和政府运营。 有一天你上传了一个视频，第二天你就被禁止了，你的账户也被删除了。 即使没有理由，他们也可以这样做。 他们拥有您和我们的数据。

我们才刚刚开始，我们还有很多悬而未决的问题，比如如何防止用户通过中央授权上传有害内容。 我很乐观，我们会在未来找到解决方案，因为我在这个领域看到了很多优秀和有才华的人。 这是关于用户的。

社区是关键

在每个领域都是如此。 我认为在 Web3 中尤其如此。 在我完全投入 Web3 之前的几周，我开始每天使用 Twitter。 这是我迄今为止做出的最好的决定之一。 我在那里遇到了很多善良，乐于助人和有才华的人！ 我今天称为朋友的人。

你必须知道我是一个更内向的人，但在科技推特上，我喜欢与人互动和交谈。 我知道 Twitter 可能非常有毒，但 Tech Twitter 是 100% 棒极了。 每个人都试图帮助、激励、教导和传播快乐。

尽管 Web3 相当新，但您已经可以找到许多可以学习的资源。 这对我来说是压倒性的。 哪一个是最好的？ 哪一个适合我？

学习平台和社区

然后我找到了 LearnWeb3DAO（Twitter： @LearnWeb3DAO ）。 我无法表达我有多高兴找到这个平台和这个社区。

首先，它们提供了一个平台，您可以在其中学习 Web3，从最开始到非常高级的主题。 一切都在你的步伐。

他们的网站： https ://www.learnweb3.io/

现在，他们提供 4 个核心曲目：

  • 大一新生
  • 二年级
  • 初级
  • 高级的

这正是我所寻找的。 从一开始就有结构化的学习路径。

每个轨道都有多个级别，您可以在其中学习区块链的核心概念，它们还为您提供了很好的资源。

最重要的是，您可以访问他们的 Discord 服务器。 这是我进入 Web3 时所做的最好的事情。 在撰写本文时，我们已经达到了 19.000 多名会员。 有成千上万的人正在学习 Web3，有些人甚至已经在这个行业工作。 每当您遇到困难并有疑问时，都会有人可以帮助您。

此外，我们每个月都会举办多个活动和社区电话会议。 例如，上个月我们举办了一个活动，多人向我们展示了他们的项目。 或者我们有一个赠品活动，人们可以赢取奖品。 但最重要的部分是人。 当我说它真的像一个家庭时，请相信我。

我怎么强调加入和参与社区的重要性都不为过。

当然，您可以自己做所有事情，并且您可能会成功。 但这需要更长的时间，会更难，而且你不会有这么多的乐趣。 社区是关键！

学习基础知识

如果您是一名开发人员并且熟悉 HTML、CSS 和 JS，那么您已经成功了一半！ 这是一个补充。 这就像 Typescript 和 Javascript。 在这种情况下真的很神奇，改变世界的功能。

有很多很棒的资源可以用来学习 Web 开发基础知识和更多关于它的博客文章。 我们生活在一个您可以仅从互联网上了解发展的世界。 那是免费的。 如果有一门课程我会推荐从头开始学习 Web 开发，那就是：
区块链和 Solidity

现在是时候深入研究区块链开发了。 你可能听说过以太坊，它肯定是最著名的区块链。 这是我建议开始的一个。 生态系统很棒，你会找到大量的资源。 社区很大而且非常好。

对于以太坊，你需要学习 Solidity。 即使您不想自己编写智能合约，了解幕后发生的事情也非常重要。 在我看来，学习编程语言的最好方法是通过编码。

多么酷啊？ 它写得非常好，易于理解，您可以通过构建游戏来练习。

资源

您可以使用大量资源来了解 Web3 并深入了解它。 我想你已经可以想象我的第一资源是什么了。 是的！ 它的LearnWeb3DAO。 我想我已经说过为什么它很棒了。 但我们还有更多！

以下是一些出色的视频内容创作者：

帕特里克·柯林斯 ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/PatrickCollins

智能合约程序员⇒ https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCJWh7F3AFyQ_x01VKzr9eyA

链环⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/chainlink

炼金术⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/AlchemyPlatform

奥斯汀格里菲斯⇒ https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC_HI2i2peo1A-STdG22GFsA

白板加密⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/WhiteboardCrypto

纳德达比特⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/naderdabit

推特也很棒。 尤其是科技推特。 尤其是 Web3 技术推特！

这里有一些您可以关注的非常好的 Web3 内容创建者。 它们都提供了持续而优质的内容：

参数=> 每日 Web3 内容，惊人的线程

我可以在这里列出 100 多人，但现在，这应该是一个很好的起点。 如果您在这个泡泡中活跃，您将立即与所有其他人联系！

构建项目

作为开发人员，最好的学习方式是实际构建项目。 您将在 LearnWeb3DAO 的 Tracks 中执行此操作，但您也应该自己构建一些东西。 如果您浏览 Youtube，您会发现很多很多教程，您将在其中构建 NFT 市场、简单的代币、NFT 集合、DAO 等。

一开始，这非常好，因为你会学到很多东西，因为创建者会解释代码和发生了什么。

但不要陷入教程地狱。 自己建造东西。

许多人（包括我自己）一开始都在努力构建一个没有教程的项目。 这是完全正常的。 我的一个建议是学习教程，在完成教程项目后，您可以使用自己的功能对其进行扩展。

您将获得自信，这将对您有很大帮助，同时完全靠自己构建一些东西。
你不必发明轮子。 只需制作多个有趣的小项目或一个您热爱的更大的项目。

慢慢来

您不必费尽心思，辞掉工作，直接跳入 Web3。 慢慢开始，并参与 Twitter 和 Discord。 只是好奇，总是愿意学习新东西。 每一天。 这需要时间。 有些人每天可以投入一小时，有些人可以每天投入 8 小时。 我有一份全职工作和一个家庭。 我每天早上都起得很早（周末也是如此），花时间学习和构建 Web3，并在 Twitter 和 Discord 上与人们互动。

每个人都有不同的情况。 找到你的方式，明智地利用你的时间，你就会进入这个行业。 迟早。



原文:

Hello there! My name is Dany and I'm a Full Stack Web Developer and currently transitioning from Web2 to Web3. My goal is to work in Web3 Full-Time and put all my time to make the Web more decentralized.

I'm on this journey for about 4 months now and want to share my story and my tips with you. You may heard of this thing called Web3, about decentralization, Crypto, Blockchain, and NFTs. Maybe you also ask yourself “Should I get into Web3” or “How i can get into Web3”.

These are the questions I want to answer right here, right now.

It's important to note that this guide is especially for people that are already in the tech space and want to transition. For people that want to get started in tech, I will do a separate post in the future!

Agenda

  • Find a reason
  • Community is key
  • Learn the fundamentals
  • Resources
  • Build Projects

For me, this is the most important step. If you find a reason you will never lose motivation. And you will need Motivation in a space that changes every single day. Web3 is still very early. Something you learned last week could be obsolete by now. If you lack motivation this could break you.

My motivation is decentralization. My goal is to fully decentralize our web for one simple reason. I want that the internet is run by us, the users, and not by big tech companies and the government. Platforms like YouTube, TikTok, etc. can do whatever they want with your account. One day you upload a video, the next day you are banned and your account is deleted. They can do this even without a reason. They own you and our data.

Web3 wants to change this. Web3 is a vision that the users own their data and no one is excluded. We are just at the beginning and we have many open questions, like how to prevent users to upload harmful content with a central authority. I'm optimistic that we will find a solution for this in the future because I see so many nice and talented people in this space. Web3 isn't about money or scam. It's about the user.

Community is key

This is true in every field. I think it's especially true in Web3. A few weeks before I dived into Web3 completely I started to use Twitter on a daily basis. It was one of the best decisions I made so far. I met so many nice, helpful and talented people out there! People that I call friends today.

You have to know I'm more of an introverted person but on Tech Twitter I love to engage and talk to people. I know that Twitter can be very toxic but Tech Twitter is 100% awesome. Everyone tries to help, to motivate, teach, and to spread happiness.

Although Web3 is pretty new you can already find many resources from where you can learn. It was overwhelming for me. Which one is the best? Which one suits me?

Learning Platform and a community

Then i found LearnWeb3DAO (Twitter: @LearnWeb3DAO). I can't express how happy I am to found this platform and this community.

First of all, they offer a platform where you can learn Web3, from the very beginning to very advanced topics. All on your pace.

Their website: https://www.learnweb3.io/

Right now they offer their 4 core tracks:

  • Freshman
  • Sophomore
  • Junior
  • Senior

That was exactly what I searched for. A structured learning path from the very beginning.

Each track has multiple levels where you learn the core concepts of Blockchain and they also provide nice resources for you.

On top of that, you can access their Discord Server. And that was the best thing I did while getting into Web3. At the time of writing this, we have reached over 19.000 Members. There are thousands of people that are learning Web3 and some even already working in this industry. Whenever you are stuck and have questions there will be someone who can help you.

Also, we have multiple events and community calls each month. For example last month we had an event where multiple people showed us their projects. Or we had a Giveaway Event where people can win prizes. But the most important part is the people. Believe me when I say that it's really like a family.

I can't emphasize enough how important it is to join and participate in a community.

Sure you could do everything on your own and you may succeed. But it will take longer, it will be harder and you will not have this much fun. Community is key!

Learn the fundamentals

If you are a developer and familiar with HTML, CSS, and JS you are halfway there! Web3 is not something that is completely new and will replace Web2. It's an addition. It's like Typescript and Javascript. Typescript adds nice features. In this case really amazing, world-changing features.

There are many great resources out there to learn Web Development fundamentals and more blog posts about it. We live in a world where you can learn about development just from the internet. And that for free. If there is one course I would recommend for learning Web Development from scratch it would be that:
https://www.udemy.com/course/the-complete-web-developer-zero-to-mastery/

Blockchain and Solidity

Now it's time to dive into Blockchain development. You may heard of Ethereum and it's for sure the most well-known Blockchain. And it's the one I would suggest to start with. The ecosystem is fantastic and you will find a ton of resources for it. The community is quite big and really nice.

For Ethereum, you need to learn Solidity. Even if you don't want to write Smart Contracts on your own it's really important to understand what's happening under the hood. In my opinion, the best way to learn a programming language is through coding.

https://cryptozombies.io/ is a website where you will learn basic and advanced Solidity by building a Zombie Game. How cool is that? It's really well written, easy to understand and you can practice by building a game.

Resources

There are a ton of resources you can use to learn about Web3 and get into it. I guess you can already imagine what is my number one resource. Yes! Its LearnWeb3DAO. I think I already said why it's awesome. But we have more!

Here are some great video content creators:

Patrick Collins ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/PatrickCollins

Smart Contract Programmer ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCJWh7F3AFyQ_x01VKzr9eyA

Chainlink ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/chainlink

Dapp University ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/DappUniversity

Alchemy ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/AlchemyPlatform

Blockgeeks ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/BlockGeeks

EatTheBlocks ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/EatTheBlocks

Austin Griffith ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC_HI2i2peo1A-STdG22GFsA

Whiteboard Crypto ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/WhiteboardCrypto

Nader Dabit ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/naderdabit

Twitter is also awesome. Especially Tech Twitter. And especially Web3 Tech Twitter!

Here are some very nice Web3 content creators you can follow. They all provide constant and good content:

Abel => Awesome content, amazing threads

Kacie => One of LearnWeb3DAO founders, working in Web3

Haardik => One of LearnWeb3DAO founders, working in Web3

Sneh => One of LearnWeb3DAO founders, working in Web3

Peter => Daily Web3 content and an amazing person

Oscar => Provides Web3 content on a daily basis and explains it very well

Balddev => I love his content, always about Web3 and how to create a better web

Nader Dabit => A legend, already working in Web3 for me a must-follow

Vitto => Super sympathetic guy, also working in Web3

Rahat => Amazing Web3 and mostly Polygon Content

Anish => Talented young developer and share his learnings

Param => Daily Web3 Content, amazing threads

Suhail => Amazing young developer, Solidity Pro

Francesco => His threads are awesome, Web3 enthusiasts and Docker Captain

Etisha => Amazing content, super sympathetic, know how to provide value

Krishna => Provides amazing Web3 content

I could list over 100 people here but for now, this should be a good starting point. And if you are active in this bubble you will connect with all the others in no time!

Build Projects

As developers, the best way to learn is to actually build projects. You will do this in the Tracks of LearnWeb3DAO but you should also build something on your own. If you go through Youtube you will find many, many tutorials where you will build NFT Marketplaces, a simple Token, NFT Collection, DAOs, etc.

In the beginning, this is excellent because you will learn a lot because the creator will explain the code and what's going on.

But don't get stuck in tutorial hell. Build things on your own.

Many people (including myself) are struggling, in the beginning, to build a project without a tutorial. That's totally normal. One tip from me would be to take tutorials and after you finished the tutorial project you extend it with your own features.

You will gain self-confidence and that will help you a lot while building something completely on your own.
You don't have to invent the wheel. Just make multiple small fun projects or maybe one bigger project that you are passionate about.

Take your time

You don't have to make a hard cut, quit your job, and jump right into Web3. Begin slowly, and engage on Twitter and Discord. Just be curious and always willing to learn new stuff. Every single day. And this will take time. Some people can invest one hour a day, some can put in 8 hours a day. I have a Full-Time Job and a family. I stand up very early every morning (Also at the weekends), to invest time to learn and build in Web3 and to engage with people on Twitter and Discord.

Everyone has different circumstances. Find your way, use your time wisely and you will get your foot into this industry. Sooner or later.
Items.Author: danytulumidis

Items.Title: 如何设置元数据 - 在已上传的对象、多个对象上设置元数据 -GCS | How to setmeta - Set metadata on already uploaded objects,multiple Objects -GCS
Items.Description:
普雷姆库马尔

发表于它需要一个或多个标头参数，后跟一个或多个 URL，其中每个标头参数采用以下两种形式之一：

 gsutil setmeta -h [header:value|header] ... url...

将元数据设置为对象如果要将缓存控制设置为“公共”，请在 Cloud Shell 中执行：

 gsutil setmeta -h "Cache-Control:public" gs://<YOUR-BUCKET-NAME>/objectName

将元数据设置为多个对象如果要将缓存控制设置为“公共”，请在 Cloud Shell 中执行：

 gsutil setmeta -h "Cache-Control:public" gs://<YOUR-BUCKET-NAME>/*.*

成为开源贡献者

克里兹蒂安·莫雷诺 - Apr 20 4月20日



原文:

PREM KUMAR

Posted on May 14

The gsutil setmeta command allows you to set or remove the metadata on one or more objects. It takes one or more header arguments followed by one or more URLs, where each header argument is in one of two forms:

gsutil setmeta -h [header:value|header] ... url...

setmeta to a object If you want to set cache-control to "public" then execute in Cloud Shell:

gsutil setmeta -h "Cache-Control:public" gs://<YOUR-BUCKET-NAME>/objectName

setmeta to multiple object If you want to set cache-control to "public" then execute in Cloud Shell:

gsutil setmeta -h "Cache-Control:public" gs://<YOUR-BUCKET-NAME>/*.*

*.* will add add the files inside the directory

Be a Opensource Contributor

Khriztian Moreno - Apr 20
Items.Author: prems5

Items.Title: 如何在学习 Web 开发时不失败 | How not to fail at learning web development
Items.Description:
许多来自外部技术的同行开始学习 Web 开发，但几个月后他们就放弃了。 我认为，如果有抱负的开发人员更多地专注于更早地寻找获利机会，这种情况就会减少。

与其做太多的课程来学习你在工作要求中看到的技能，你可以在每次更长的学习课程之后尝试赚钱。 即使有 HTML/CSS + 设计甚至产品制作选项，也有自由职业者的演出。 这是一篇详细介绍此内容的文章： https ://devreso.com/how-earn-money-html-css-plus-design/

有免费的在线表单数据捕获服务，您可以轻松地将其集成到您的 HTML/CSS 网站中，并将完整的功能项目交付给朋友或工作伙伴。 您可以制作 HTML 模板和电子书，并在您的社交媒体上使用 Gumroad 进行销售。

在每个学习阶段从你自己的项目中获得一点钱将保持动力。 这将使您能够走上旅程，直到从出色的开发工作或成功的产品中获得所需的资金。 所以我的建议是想办法将你早期学到的东西货币化，以免长期放弃。

我个人了解到，自由职业者和产品购买者不需要世界上最漂亮、最完美的产品/服务。 不，他们只是希望有人真正倾听他们的意见，并提供一个体面的结果，帮助他们实现目标。 因此，您不必害怕尽早进行货币化。

期待听到您对此的看法



原文:

Many peers coming from outside tech start to learn web development and in a couple of months they give up. I think this would happen less often if aspiring developers would focus more on finding monetization opportunities earlier.

Instead of doing too many courses to learn the skills you see in job requirements you can try to monetize after each longer learning session. There are freelancing gigs even with HTML/CSS + design and even product making options. Here is an article with details on this: https://devreso.com/how-earn-money-html-css-plus-design/

There are free online form data capture services that you can easily integrate into your HTML/CSS website and deliver a complete functional project to a friend or work mate. You can make HTML templates and ebooks and sell them with Gumroad on your social media.

Getting a little bit of money after each learning stage from your own projects will keep the motivation pumping. This will enable you to walk the journey until reaching the desired money from a great dev job or from a successful product. So my recommendation is to find ways to monetize what you learn early in order to not give up on the long term.

I've personally learned that freelance customers and product buyers don't require the most beautiful and well polished product/service in the world. No. They just want for someone to actually listen to them and deliver a decent result that will help them meet their objectives. So you shouldn't be scared to go for monetization early.

Looking forward to hear your thoughts on this

Items.Title: 让我们在 React JSX 中修复三元组 | Let’s repair ternary in React JSX
Items.Description:
据说三元运算符 (x ? y : z) 从进入 React JSX 的那一天起就会触发有文化的程序员的强迫症。 这是当前有条件地渲染 React 组件的事实上的方式。 如果我们抽象出这种奇怪的语法并有一个友好的界面来有条件地渲染事物会怎样。

让我们启动一个小型 Next.js 应用程序，如果生成的随机数是偶数，它会在屏幕上显示一条消息。

在以下代码段中，您将看到使用三元运算符进行渲染的常用方法：

这是我们在忍者清洁任务中的专用组件：


重构和快乐的代码。

结论

如果不出意外，我们所取得的成就就是“善待眼睛”。 此外，现在我们有了一种标准方法，可以使用这个可重用组件在整个应用程序中处理条件渲染。 好吧，回想起来，我们通过编写三元组来摆脱三元组似乎很有趣。 讽刺！

将发布有关各种日常黑客的此类微文章。 敬请关注！

连接

========== ==========

🏭 LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sameerkumar1612 🏭 领英： https ://www.linkedin.com/in/sameerkumar1612

✍️ Follow on Medium: https://sameer-kumar-1612.medium.com ✍️ 关注媒体： https ://sameer-kumar-1612.medium.com



原文:

The ternary operator (x ? y : z) is said to trigger OCD in well-cultured programmers from the day it came into React JSX. It is the current defacto way of rendering react components conditionally. What if we abstract this weird syntax and have a friendly interface to render things conditionally.

Let's spin up a small Next.js application which shows a message on screen if the generated random number is even.

In the following snippet, you’ll see the common way to render using the ternary operator:

🙁 The funny and weird syntax presentation from lines 9 to 13 kills my mood every time I see it.

Here comes our dedicated component on the ninja cleaning mission:


Refactored and Happy code.

Conclusion

==========

What we achieved is “Treat to eyes” if nothing else. Also, now we have a standard way of handling conditional rendering throughout the application using this reusable component. Well, looking back, it seems funny that we got rid of ternary by writing a ternary. Irony!

Will be posting such micro articles on various day-to-day hacks. Stay tuned!

To Connect

==========

✍️ Follow on Medium: https://sameer-kumar-1612.medium.com

Embora nem semper seja o caso，a complexidade ciclomática pode ser um dos fatores que impulsionam a complexidade cognitiva。 Quanto maior a complexidade cognitiva de um pedaço de código, mais difícil é navegar e manter. 原文:

Neste post vamos aprender o que é complexidade ciclomática, como identificá-la e quais os seus pontos negativos. Vamos usar bastante o Credo para identificar de fato a complexidade de cada exemplo fornecido.

Para este artigo foi usado como base How To Reduce Cyclomatic Complexity: A Complete Guide

O que é complexidade ciclomática?

A complexidade ciclomática (CC) é uma métrica de complexidade de software intimamente correlacionada com erros de codificação.

Se uma função parece que ficou muito complexa, na maioria das vezes ela também tem um valor CC alto. Portanto, isso é útil para convencer os membros da equipe e os chefes da necessidade de refatorar partes do código com base em métricas "objetivas".

A complexidade ciclomática é o número mínimo de casos de teste necessários para alcançar a cobertura total da ramificação. Assim, a complexidade ciclomática pode prever o quão difícil é testar um determinado pedaço de código.

Vamos construir um projeto para testar alguns exemplos.

Criando o projeto

O projeto pronto você encontra em: https://github.com/maiquitome/cyclomatic_complexity

mix phx.new cyclomatic_complexity

Quem verifica o CC é o Credo, então instale ele adicionando a linha abaixo ao seu mix.exs:

{:credo, "~> 1.6", only: [:dev, :test], runtime: false}

e rodando o comando abaixo no terminal:

Exemplos de CC simples

Vamos criar uns exemplos bem simples de complexidade ciclomática e, por serem bem simples, vamos precisar configurar o Credo para identificar o CC delas.

Rode o comando abaixo no terminal para gerar o arquivo de configuração .credo.exs:

Agora no arquivo .credo.exs você pode configurar o max_complexity ao seu gosto (o padrão é 9). Vamos configurar o credo para aceitar apenas a complexidade 1, assim vai ser mais fácil criarmos exemplos, pois se a complexidade for maior que 1, o credo irá gerar um alerta para refatoração:

{Credo.Check.Refactor.CyclomaticComplexity, [max_complexity: 1]}

Você pode achar sobre isso na documentação do credo.

Função de complexidade 1

Como tem uma única declaração, é fácil ver que sua complexidade ciclomática é 1.

Função de complexidade 2

Para a mensagem aparecer dessa forma no seu VSCode instale as extensões Error Lens e Linter.

A segunda versão da função tem uma ramificação. O chamador para a função pode passar true como o valor para o parâmetro say_goodbye, mesmo que o valor padrão seja false. Se isso acontecer, a função imprimirá uma mensagem de adeus após dizer olá. Por outro lado, se o chamador não fornecer um valor para o parâmetro ou escolher false, a mensagem de despedida não será exibida.

Assim, a função tem duas ramificações de execução possíveis, o que é o mesmo que dizer que ela tem um valor de complexidade ciclomática de 2.

Função de complexidade 3

Vamos aumentar a complexidade para 3:

Agora existem três ramificações de execução possíveis.

Reduzindo a CC

Vamos reduzir essa complexidade ciclomática de 3 para 1. Em Elixir conseguimos fazer isso facilmente:

Agora não temos mais o alerta do Credo, pois a complexidade ciclomática agora é de 1. Lembrando que o padrão do Credo é 9, então não precisamos reduzir sempre para 1, senão não conseguiríamos nunca usar uma estrutura de decisão como o case, cond, with.

Problemas futuros

A complexidade ciclomática não é intrinsecamente ruim. Por exemplo, você pode ter um pedaço de código com um valor complexo ciclomático um tanto alto que é super fácil de ler e entender.

No entanto, de modo geral, podemos dizer que ter uma complexidade ciclomática muito alta é um sintoma de problemas com a base de código ou uma causa potencial de problemas futuros. Vamos abordar algumas das razões pelas quais você deseja reduzi-la com mais detalhes.

Complexidade cognitiva

A complexidade cognitiva refere-se a quão difícil é entender um determinado pedaço de código. Embora nem sempre seja o caso, a complexidade ciclomática pode ser um dos fatores que impulsionam a complexidade cognitiva. Quanto maior a complexidade cognitiva de um pedaço de código, mais difícil é navegar e manter.

Código mais difícil de testar

Valores mais altos de complexidade ciclomática resultam na necessidade de um número maior de casos de teste para testar de forma abrangente um bloco de código – por exemplo, uma função. Portanto, se você deseja facilitar sua vida ao escrever testes, provavelmente deseja reduzir a complexidade ciclomática do seu código.

Maior risco de defeitos

É provável que você introduza defeitos em uma área da base de código que você altera muito do que em uma área onde raramente toca. Além disso, quanto mais complexo for um determinado trecho de código, maior a probabilidade de você o interpretar mal e introduzir um defeito nele.

Conclusão

Concluímos que a complexidade ciclomática (CC) nos ajuda a ter um código mais fácil de entender, mais fácil de testar e de dar manutenção; evitando problemas futuros. Não precisamos reduzir a CC para 1, mas precisamos ficar atentos a esses fatores.

Items.Description:
在本文中，我们将看到 15 个以上的高级 Python 概念，如果您是 Python 开发人员，您必须了解这些概念。 如果您想学习 Python 编程或者您是 Python 开发人员，那么您必须了解这些 Python 高级概念。 所以让我们开始这篇文章，不要浪费任何时间：
目录

一个通用的 python 函数是使用 def function_name() 的样式定义的，但是对于 lambda 函数，我们不给它一个名字。 映射函数的语法：

地图（函数，可迭代）

过滤器也是一个 python 内置函数，与 Map 函数非常相似。 过滤函数的语法：

过滤器（功能，itertools）

生成器函数用于允许您声明一个行为类似于迭代器的函数，它还允许程序员以简单快速的方式制作迭代器。 生成器是 Python 编程中的一种特殊类型的函数，它不返回单个值，而是返回一个可迭代的生成器对象。 在生成器中，使用函数 yield 语句而不是 return 语句。

谢谢
法典



原文:

Python is an object-oriented and high-level programming language and getting more popular nowadays because of its dynamic nature, versatility, and ease to learn. Python is the second most preferred programming language after JavaScript and it can be used in any field like Artificial Intelligence, Machine learning, Deep learning, and Web Development.

In this article, we are going to see 15+ advanced python concepts that you must know if you are a python developer. If you want to learn python programming or you are a python developer then you must know these python advanced concepts. So let's start this article without wasting any time:
Table Of Contents

1. lambda Function

A lambda is a small anonymous function that can take any number of arguments but can only have one expression. A general python function is defined using the style of def function_name(), but with the lambda function, we don't give it a name. The Lambda function is very important because the purpose of this function is to perform some kind of simple and operational expression without the need for fully defining a function.

The Syntax of Lambda Function:

lambda arguments : expression

2. Map Function

Python programming has a built-in function called map() which returns a map object(which is an iterator) of the results after applying the given function to each item of a given iterable(tuple, list or dict, etc.). The map function is most importantly a readable way to perform such an operation.

The syntax for Map Function:

map(function, iterable)

3. Filtering

The filter is also a python built-in function which is quite similar to the Map function. The main difference between map() and filter() is that the filter function only returns the filtered elements as True from a given sequence.

The Syntax of Filter Function:

filter(function, itertools)

4. Generator

A generator function is used to allow you to declare a function that behaves like an iterator and it also allows programmers to make an iterator in an easy and fast way. A generator is a special type of function in python programming which does not return a single value instead it returns an iterable generator object. In the generator, the function yield statement is used rather than the return statement.

Click Here to read more

Thankyou

Items.Title: 云成本对您的业务构成风险：这是保护自己的方法 | Cloud Costs Are a Risk to Your Business: Here’s How to Protect Yourself
Items.Description:
对于大流行后世界中的每个现代组织来说，云都是一项重要的预算项目。 但有时，它的成本变得太大而无法舒适。 一家价值 10 亿美元的私有软件公司告诉Andreessen Horowitz ，其公共云支出占总收入成本 (COR) 的 81% 。

虽然上面的例子是一个极端的例子，但它仍然反映了许多软件公司的挣扎。

如果他们首先处理好他们的云支出，也许我们目前看到的所有裁员都不会发生到这样的程度？ 在上面的示例中，人工成本不超过 19%，因此仅削减四分之一的云账单就比让所有员工离开更容易节省。

幸运的是，有一条出路。 但让我们首先检查问题的根源。

为什么云会带来财务风险？

随着云使用量的增长并主导 IT 预算（预计到2025 年将达到 51% ），组织争相降低费用。 他们的第一步通常是预先购买特定数量和/或类型的云服务，以从折扣中受益（预留实例）。

但这样做时，公司基本上假设他们的许多业务变量在预订期间不会改变。

这往往与现实不符。 承诺的云支出通常比实际支出低约 20% ，一些公司最终超出了他们最初的估计 2 倍！

业界的故事证实了这一点。 在一个假期期间，Pinterest 的云支出超出了最初的估计约 2000 万美元。

因此，即使您通过承诺在一年或三年的合同中使用云资源来获得折扣，您也可能得不到足够的折扣。

或者，当人们在不再需要时忘记关闭资源或更糟糕的是，运行一项工作而忘记它时，宝贵的折扣容量可能会被浪费。 在任何一种情况下，您最终都会为无法转化为您的业务价值的资源付费。 接下来你知道的是，这些隐藏成本开始累积并成为重大的财务风险。

假设您正在运行一个本地数据中心。 如果您购买服务器，则需要固定成本和特定的运营费用。 您可以制定预算并坚持下去。

但是，当您开始使用 Amazon EC2 等公共云服务来运行您的应用程序时，预测成本会变得很棘手。 您的团队可以轻松地在给定时间点运行 100 或 1000 台服务器，这尤其具有挑战性，因为只需单击几下即可启动这些新虚拟机。

人们可能会为任务运行服务器并在工作完成后忘记它们。 但是你会继续为他们买单，积累大量的财务浪费。

定价模型的复杂性

大多数公共云提供商在多个页面和长表格中展示他们的定价。 使用不同的指标对服务收费，例如使用时间、数据量、计算能力和数据传输。

每个价格因素都会随着云区域、实例类型或整体数据量等维度发生变化。

最重要的是，云账单很长，所有成本数据都有延迟。 你在这里处理很多复杂性。

供应商锁定

许多公司最终只使用一个云提供商来处理多个工作负载，从而有效地将自己锁定在该提供商的定价中。 没问题，直到出现意外的价格上涨，比如 谷歌最近提高了定价。 其他供应商也可能效仿。

供应商锁定会给您带来许多不必要的问题，从替代服务中错失的机会到您正在使用的解决方案被淘汰的风险。

出路

如果您想让您的公司在未来抵御这些风险，请从降低运营成本开始。 公共云费用是一个很好的选择，因为市场上到处都是解决方案，可以帮助您在不付出太多额外努力的情况下削减您的云费用。

通过自动化与管理和优化云成本相关的所有任务，您可以重新控制资源，而无需雇用额外的工程师。



原文:

The cloud makes a big budget item for every modern organization in the post-pandemic world. But sometimes, its costs become too big for comfort. A billion-dollar private software company told Andreessen Horowitz that its public cloud spend made up 81% of the total cost of revenue (COR).

While the example above is an extreme case, it still reflects the struggle of many software companies. 

Perhaps all the layoffs we’re currently seeing wouldn’t be happening to such an extent if they took care of their cloud spend first? In the example above, labor cost is not more than 19%, so cutting a mere quarter of the cloud bill would easily save more than letting their entire staff go. 

Luckily, there’s a way out. But let’s examine the root of the problem first.

Why does the cloud come with a financial risk?

As their cloud usage grows and dominates IT budgets (expected to hit 51% by 2025), organizations scramble to lower their bills. Their first move is often to pre-purchase a specific amount and/or type of cloud services to benefit from discounts (reserved instances). 

But when doing that, companies essentially assume many of their business variables won’t change during the reservation period. 

This often doesn’t match the reality. Committed cloud spend is usually ~20% lower than actual spend, and some companies end up going over their initial estimations by 2x!

Stories from the industry confirm this. During one holiday season, Pinterest’s cloud spend went beyond the initial estimates by some $20 million.

So, even if you snatch a discount by committing to use cloud resources within a one- or three-year contract, you might not get enough of it. 

Or the precious discounted capacity might get wasted when people forget to shut resources down when no longer needed or worse, run a job and forget about it. That happened to Adobe which racked up an unplanned cloud bill of over 500k because of a computing workload left running on Azure. 

In either case, you end up paying for resources that don’t translate into any value for your business. Next thing you know, these hidden costs start adding up and become a significant financial risk.

3 biggest cloud cost risks

Imagine that you’re running an on-premises data center. If you buy a server, it comes with a fixed cost and specific operating expense. You can create your budget and stick to it.

But predicting costs gets tricky when you start using public cloud services like Amazon EC2 to run your applications. Your team can easily run 100 or 1000 servers at a given point in time, which is especially challenging because spinning up these new virtual machines takes a few clicks. 

People might run servers for tasks and forget about them once the job is done. But you’ll keep paying for them, accumulating massive financial waste. 

The complexity of pricing models

Most public cloud providers present their pricing across multiple pages and long tables. Services are charged using different metrics like time of use, data volumes, compute capacity, and data transfer.

And each price factor changes across dimensions like cloud region, instance type, or overall data volume. 

On top of that, cloud bills are long, and all the cost data comes with a delay. You’re dealing with a lot of complexity here.

Vendor lock-in

Many companies end up using only one cloud provider for multiple workloads, effectively locking themselves into the pricing of that provider. No issue, until a surprise price hike happens, like Google raising its pricing recently. And other providers might as well follow.

Vendor lock-in brings you many unwanted issues, from missed opportunities in alternative services to the risk of having the solution you’re using sunsetted. 

The way out

If you want to future-proof your company against these risks, start by reducing your operating costs. Public cloud expenses are a great candidate for this because the market is full of solutions that can help slash your cloud bill without much extra effort.

By automating all the tasks related to managing and optimizing your cloud costs, you gain back control over your resources without having to hire additional engineers.

Items.Title: Remix Markdown 概览页面 | Remix Markdown overview page
Items.Description:
现在我们已经有了Markdown 支持的页面，让我们看看如何添加某种概览页面。

这可以在您的主页上展示您的最新文章，或者只是一个博客概述页面。

注意：在做研究时，我找不到自动化的方法，所以一旦我找到了这篇文章可能会更新。

加载 Markdown 文件

我不是 100% 确定在撰写本文时是否有一种自动化的方法。

如果我找到了，我会更新这篇文章🙌。

让我们开始吧！

在我们的示例中，我们将创建一个博客概览页面，因此让我们在blog目录中添加index.tsx文件。

现在我们可以从加载我们想要显示的降价文件开始。

 import * as firstArticle from ' ./my-first-article.md ' ; import * as secondArticle from ' ./mdx-sample.mdx ' ;

然后我们只想加载 Markdown 文件的元数据（Frontmatter 部分）。 因此，让我们创建一个只提取该信息而不是整个文件的函数。 

 function postFromModule ( mod ) { return { slug : mod . filename . replace ( / \. mdx ? $/ , '' ), ... mod . attributes . meta , }; }

此函数将返回文件的 slug 和所有 frontmatter 部分。
然后我们可以使用 Remix loader 来实际加载这些页面。

 export async function loader () { return json ([ postFromModule ( firstArticle ), postFromModule ( secondArticle )]); }

现在我们可以在我们的页面中访问这些数据。

 export default function Index () { const posts = useLoaderData (); return ( < ul > { posts . map (( post ) => ( < li key = { post . slug } > < Link to = { post . slug } > { post . title } < /Link > { post . description ? < p > { post . description } < /p> : null } < /li > ))} < /ul > ); }

就是这样。 查看此GitHub 存储库以查看完整代码。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

Now that we have our Markdown powered page to work, let's see how we can add some kind of overview page.

This could be on your homepage to showcase your latest articles or simply a blog overview page.

Note: While doing research, I could not find an automated way, so this article might get updated once I do.

Loading Markdown files

Markdown files have to be loaded individually in Remix. I'm not 100% sure if there is an automated way of doing it at the point of writing.

If I do find one, I'll update this article 🙌.

Let's get started!

In our example, we'll create a blog overview page, so let's add the index.tsx file inside our blog directory.

Now we can start by loading the markdown files we want to show.

import * as firstArticle from './my-first-article.md';
import * as secondArticle from './mdx-sample.mdx';

Then we only want to load the metadata (Frontmatter section) of the Markdown files. So let's create a function that only extracts that information rather than the whole file.

function postFromModule(mod) { return { slug: mod.filename.replace(/\.mdx?$/, ''), ...mod.attributes.meta, };
}

This function will return the slug and all frontmatter parts of the file.
The frontmatter section is everything within the three dashes at the top of the file.

Then we can use the Remix loader to actually load these pages.

export async function loader() { return json([postFromModule(firstArticle), postFromModule(secondArticle)]);
}

Now we can access this data inside our page.

export default function Index() { const posts = useLoaderData(); return ( <ul> {posts.map((post) => ( <li key={post.slug}> <Link to={post.slug}>{post.title}</Link>
 {post.description ? <p>{post.description}</p> : null}
 </li>
 ))} </ul>
 );
}

And that's it. Remix will loop over the posts we have defined in our import and show the title and description with a link to the actual page.

Check out this GitHub repo to see the complete code.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

Items.Description:
阿尔瓦罗·蒙托罗

发表于作为comiCSS webcomic的一部分，今天我编写了一个关于 FlexBox 和 justify-content 值的卡通：

我试图做一些不同的事情并以有趣的方式解释这个概念，说明justify-content的主要选项：

  • 此外，连环画假定默认的flex-direction为row 。 如果我们将值更改为column ，卡通人物看起来更像毕加索😅

    下一个网格......也许？

本杰明·C·贝克 - May 12 5月12日

Jan Schenk (he/him) - Jan Schenk (他/他) - May 9 5月9日



原文:

Alvaro Montoro

Posted on May 14

As part of the comiCSS webcomic, today I coded a cartoon about FlexBox and the values of justify-content:

I was trying to do something different and explain the concept in a fun way, illustrating the main options for justify-content:

  • flext-start: pack items at the beginning (by default, the left side).
  • flex-end: pack the items at the end (right side).
  • center: pack the items in the center.
  • space-evenly: all items will have the same space around them (even with the start and end of the container.)
  • space-around: the space between objects is the same, and the area at the beginning and end of the container will be half of that space.
  • space-between: the distance between all items is equal, with the first and last item touching the container edges.

There are more options for justify-content (stretch, left, right, start, end, and different combinations with safe and unsafe.) But I had to keep the cartoon short and straightforward, plus they wouldn’t be much different from some of the others.

Also, the comic strip assumes the default flex-direction of row. If we changed the value to column, the cartoon character would look more like a Picasso 😅

Next grid... maybe?

Benjamin C. Baker - May 12

Jan Schenk (he/him) - May 9

Items.Title: 如何在 vue3 中优雅地使用 BEM | How to elegantly use BEM in vue3
Items.Description:
安装

npm i vue3-bem

使用

如下所示。

 // .vue import useBem from " vue3-bem " ; const bem = useBem ( " block " );
 <div :class= "bem('elem', 'selected')" ></div>
.block { &__elem { &--selected { } } }

使用useBem设置块。
使用bem配置元素和修饰符以返回类。

 type BemFunction = function ( e ?: string | null , m ?: string | string [] | { [ key in string ]: boolean } ) => string []; useBem : function ( block : string ) => BemFunction ; bem : BemFunction

工具

如果你觉得每个组件都写 import 比较麻烦，可以使用插件vite-plugin-vue3-bem这样就不需要写 import vue3-bem 。

使用vite-plugin-vue3-bem可以帮助你删除导入。

 // like it < script lang = " ts " bem - block = " tip " > < /script > // equivalent to < script lang = " ts " bem - block = " tip " > import useBem from " ./useBem " ; const bem = useBem ( ' tip ' ); < /script >
 // vite.config.js import ViteVue3Bem from " vite-plugin-vue3-bem " ; plugins :[ ViteVue3Bem () ]

类型声明 typescript 中需要类型声明以防止错误报告。 

 // env.d.ts import " vue3-bem " ;

例子

自定义块名称

<div class= "tip" > <div :class= "bem(" wrap ")" ></div> </div>
 < script setup > import useBem from " ./useBem " ; const bem = useBem ( ' tip ' ); < /script >

内联修饰符

<div :class= "bem('container')" > <div :class= "bem(" header ")" ></div> <div :class= "bem('item', [" selected ", " highlighted "])" ></div> </div>
 < script setup > import useBem from " ./useBem " ; const bem = useBem ( ' tip ' ); < /script >
.tip { &__header { } &__item { &--selected { } &--highlighted { } } }

条件修饰符

<div :class= "bem('container')" > <div :class= "bem(" header ")" ></div> <div :class= "bem('item', {" selected " : true , " highlighted " : highlighted })" ></div> </div>
 < script > import useBem from " ./useBem " ; const bem = useBem ( ' tip ' ); const highlighted = ref ( false ); < /script >
.tip { &__header { } &__item { &--selected { } &--highlighted { } } }

如果你认为它对你有帮助，请给一颗星。

github: vue3-bem github: vue3-bem



原文:

Simple implementation of BEM in Vue 3.x, helps you use the BEM specification more concisely in vue3.

Installation

npm i vue3-bem

Using

vue3-bem is also very easy to use. As shown below.

// .vue
import useBem from "vue3-bem";
const bem = useBem("block");

<div :class="bem('elem', 'selected')"></div>

.block {
  &__elem {
    &--selected {
    }
  }
}

Api

Use useBem to set the block.
Use bem to configure elements and modfiers to return classes.

type BemFunction = function ( e?: string | null, m?: string | string[] | { [key in string]: boolean }
) => string[]; useBem: function (block: string) => BemFunction; bem: BemFunction

Tools

If you think it's too much trouble to write import for each component, you can use the plugin vite-plugin-vue3-bem so you don't need to write import vue3-bem.

Use vite-plugin-vue3-bem can help you remove import.

// like it
<script lang="ts" bem-block="tip">
</script>

// equivalent to
<script lang="ts" bem-block="tip">
import useBem from "./useBem";
const bem = useBem('tip');
</script>

// vite.config.js
import ViteVue3Bem from "vite-plugin-vue3-bem"; plugins:[ ViteVue3Bem()
]

Type Declaration Type declaration is required in typescript to prevent error reporting.

// env.d.ts
import "vue3-bem";

Example

Custom block name 

<div class="tip"> <div :class="bem("wrap")"></div>
</div>

<script setup>
import useBem from "./useBem";
const bem = useBem('tip');
</script>

Inline modfiers 

<div :class="bem('container')"> <div :class="bem("header")"></div> <div :class="bem('item', ["selected", "highlighted"])"></div>
</div>

<script setup>
import useBem from "./useBem";
const bem = useBem('tip');
</script>

.tip {
    &__header {
    }
    &__item {
        &--selected {
        }
        &--highlighted {
        }
    }
}

Conditional modfiers 

<div :class="bem('container')"> <div :class="bem("header")"></div> <div :class="bem('item', {"selected": true, "highlighted": highlighted})"></div>
</div>

<script> import useBem from "./useBem"; const bem = useBem('tip'); const highlighted = ref(false);
</script>

.tip {
    &__header {
    }
    &__item {
        &--selected {
        }
        &--highlighted {
        }
    }
}

Give a star if you think it will help you.

Items.Title: 新开发者在这里....超学习？ | new dev here ....ultra learning?
Items.Description:
发布1！
我正在建立一个博客/网站来记录我在视频游戏方面的高水平之旅。 首先从 Valorant、Apex Legends 和 Starcraft2 开始！

我将构建一个 cms 风格的博客，并使用 Vue 或其他一些运行速度更快、响应速度更快的框架。 将致力于集成一个 twitch 嵌入按钮，用于流式传输我的 twitch 流或 facebook 游戏。 人们将能够在首页上查看我是否在线。 最重要的是，我将记录对这个博客进行编码的内容和无效的内容。

就像一个二买一的交易编码之旅和爬梯之旅！！！！

按照我为自己设定的高期望，我的目标是每周至少更新一次，并记下我学到的东西。 如果不是每周3个帖子？ 然而，它是超学习，所以它的 SORTA 加速和更快。 谢谢你斯科特杨的想法。



原文:

post 1!
I am building a blog/website to document my journey to a very high level in video games. first off starting with Valorant, Apex Legends, and Starcraft2!

I will be building a cms style blog and using either Vue or some other framework that works faster and responsive. Will work on integrating a twitch embed button that streams my twitch stream or facebook gaming. people will be able to see if im online or not on the front home page. on top of that i will document what worked in coding this blog and what didnt.

ITS LIKE A TWO FOR ONE DEAL A JOURNEY OF CODING AND JOURNEY OF CLIMBING THE LADDER!!!!

going by the high expectations i set for myself i aim to at least update once a week and note down what i have learned. if not 3 posts a week? however it is ultra learning so ITS SORTA ACCELERATED AND FASTER. thank you scott young for the idea.

Items.Title: 作为程序员的毅力，对梦想和目标的承诺 | The perseverance, commitment to my dreams and goals as a programmer
Items.Description:
我厌倦了混乱，部门和客户之间的冲突（因为许多经理和主管都专注于钱）。 我有一台笔记本电脑（联想ideapad 330s）。 我组装了一张小桌子，拿了一把塑料椅子，一个便宜的 rgb 键盘/鼠标，那是我的第一个工作空间。

嘿！ 这比床好。 相信我！

后来，到那年 11 月（仍然是 2020 年），我从我的第一个自由职业者客户那里得到了一些钱。 我用这笔钱从朋友那里买了一台新显示器和一张奇怪的桌子。 那张桌子很奇怪，因为其中一张桌子展开了，你有一个 L 表工作区。 哦！ 我买了便宜的无线键盘、鼠标和耳机。 我处于“无线”趋势模式。 目前效率不高。 请忽略底部的白色外壳。 那是我的第一台 linux 服务器。 我还买了一把符合人体工程学的椅子。

我使用该设置一年。 到 2021 年底，我将我的电脑从笔记本电脑升级到了便宜的游戏电脑：

  • 锐龙 3 3300X
  • 我是一个小型UPS

我还升级了我的键盘、鼠标和耳机：

  • 带 Outemu 红色开关的键盘 Kumara k552。
  • 鼠标罗技g102
  • 一个 razer 耳机（抱歉，我现在没有型号）。

我很高兴，因为我可以更好地工作，并以更好的图形玩同样的老游戏。

一周后，我终于为我的 kumara 键盘买了一个不错的办公桌 CubiCubi 和酷炫的黑/绿键帽。 我还买了一些便宜的 rgb 扬声器。

到 2022 年 4 月底，我更换了扬声器：买了一个带低音炮的罗技扬声器、一个 LED 灯带和一盏台灯。 这是我目前的设置：

底线是：不知何故，我目前的设置是我在 2020 年开始作为自由职业者工作时的想象。而且我知道我会不断改善我的工作空间和房间。 例如，我将在下周粉刷房间。

我真的很想分享这个，因为我想告诉你，如果你有专注、信念和意志，你就能实现你的目标！



原文:

In 2020 I quit my job as a programmer in a company I was working for 8 years. I got tired of the mess, the conflicts between the departments and clients (because many managers and supervisors are focused on the money). I had a laptop (a Lenovo ideapad 330s). I assembled a small table, took a plastic chair, a cheap rgb keyboard/mouse and that was my first workspace.

Hey! It was better than the bed. Trust me!

Later, by november of that year (still 2020) I got some money from my first client as freelancer. I used the money to buy a new monitor and a weird desk from a friend. That desk is weird because one of the tables unfolds and you had like an L table workspace. (I'm sorry for my bad english). Oh! I bought cheap wireless keyboard, mouse and headphones. I was in a "Wireless" trending mode. Not very efficient at the moment. Please, ignore the white case at the bottom. That was my first linux server. I also bought an ergonomic chair.

I used that setup for a year. By the end of 2021 I upgraded my pc from the laptop to a cheap gaming PC:

  • Ryzen 3 3300X
  • Nvidia GT 730 (I know, the lowest of the lowest).
  • I a small UPS

I also upgraded my keyboard, mouse and headset:

  • Keyboard Kumara k552 with Outemu red switches.
  • Mouse logitech g102
  • A razer headset (sorry, I don't have the model right now).

I was happy because I could work better and play the same old games with better graphics.

A week later I finally bought a decent desk CubiCubi and cool black/green keycaps for my kumara keyboard. I also bought some cheap rgb speakers.

By the end of April 2022, I changed the speakers: Bought a logitech speakers with subwoofer, a led light strip and a desk lamp. This is my current setup:

Bottom line is: My current setup is somehow what I imagined when I started working as a freelancer in 2020. And I know I will keep improving my workspace and room. I will paint the room next week, for example.

I really wanted to shared this because I want to tell you that you can achieve your goals if you have focus, conviction and will!

Items.Title: 如何在 Azure 容器应用中快速设置 Let's Encrypt 证书 | How to quickly setup a Let's Encrypt certificate in Azure Container Apps
Items.Description:
现在，您可以使用自己的域轻松发布自己的 Web 应用程序，而无需使用 Front Door。

这是真的！！！ 您现在可以将自定义域添加到您的 Azure 容器应用程序！

但是，它目前不支持免费托管证书，您必须上传自己的证书。

免费证书由 Let's Encrypt 颁发，因此我开发了一个应用程序来使用它们。 这是基于 Azure 应用团队的@jeffhollan编写的代码。

这是一个自动为 Azure 容器应用程序颁发和续订 ACME SSL/TLS 证书的应用程序。

  • 支持多个容器应用程序和容器应用程序环境
  • 易于部署和配置
  • 高度可靠的实施
  • 易于监控（Application Insights、Webhook）

您可以将多个证书添加到单个容器应用程序。

目录

  • 功能支持
  • 要求
  • 入门
  • 用法
  • 谢谢
  • 执照

功能支持

  • 为 Zone Apex / 多域 / 通配符颁发证书
  • 自定义域和证书自动绑定到 Container App
  • 在单个应用程序中支持多个容器应用程序
  • 符合 ACME 的认证机构
    • 让我们加密
    • 要求
      • 电子邮件地址（在 Let's Encrypt 注册时需要）

      入门

      蔚蓝中国更新 Function App 的以下配置设置：

      • Acmebot:Webhook

它具有与其他 Acmebot 产品相同的功能，但支持 Container Apps 特定功能。 当前需要使用 Azure DNS。

从这里开始，我将实际使用 Acmebot 将自定义域和证书添加到 Container App。

部署 Acmebot

使用 GitHub 上自述文件中的“部署到 Azure”按钮部署 Acmebot。

部署完成后，使用应用服务身份验证添加 Azure AD 身份验证。 自述文件中提供了详细说明。

为 Acmebot 设置 RBAC (IAM)

将Contributor RBAC 角色添加到部署了 Container Apps 和 Container Apps Environment 的资源组的已部署 Acmebot。

目前容器应用没有 RBAC 角色，因此必须分配Contributor RBAC 角色。

由于 Acmebot 需要 Azure DNS 来颁发证书，因此也将DNS Zone Contributor的角色分配给 Acmebot 以用于 Azure DNS。

颁发证书

如果设置成功，访问应用程序将显示 DNS 区域和容器应用程序环境列表。

以下简单屏幕允许您选择 DNS 区域并为所需域名颁发证书。

如果证书颁发成功，可以在容器应用环境的证书列表中找到。

绑定到容器应用

另一个选项是在颁发证书的同时将自定义域设置添加到任何容器应用程序。

这是为了安全而设计的。

手动添加 A 记录后，浏览器将能够查看托管在 HTTPS 保护的 Zone apex 域中的 Container App，如下图所示。

通过这种方式，Acmebot 大大减少了为容器应用程序颁发证书的工作量并实现了管理自动化。

更新证书

默认设置是在证书到期前 30 天自动更新证书。

相关帖子

如果您对 Acmebot 的 App Service 和 Key Vault 版本感兴趣，请参考以下帖子。

享受 Azure 容器应用，让我们加密吧！



原文:

Azure Container Apps now supports custom domain assignment in the latest update. Now you can easily publish your own web application with your own domain without using Front Door.

IT's TRUE!!! You can now add Custom Domains to your Azure Container App!

@AzContainerApp #AzureContainerApp @nthonyChu @yosoyvinisoto

However, it does not currently support free managed certificates, and you must upload your own certificates.

Free certificates are issued by Let's Encrypt, so I developed an application to use them. This is based on code written by @jeffhollan of the Azure Apps team.

This is an application that automates the issuance and renewal of ACME SSL/TLS certificates for Azure Container Apps.

  • Support for multiple Container Apps and Container Apps Environment
  • Easy to deploy and configure
  • Highly reliable implementation
  • Ease of Monitoring (Application Insights, Webhook)

You can add multiple certificates to a single Container Apps.

Table Of Contents

  • Feature Support
  • Requirements
  • Getting Started
  • Usage
  • Thanks
  • License

Feature Support

  • Issuing certificates for Zone Apex / Multi-domain / Wildcard
  • Automatic binding of custom domains and certificates to Container App
  • Support for multiple Container Apps in a single application
  • ACME-compliant Certification Authorities
    • Let's Encrypt
    • Buypass Go SSL
    • ZeroSSL (Requires EAB Credentials)

Requirements

  • Azure Subscription
  • Azure Container Apps
  • Azure DNS
  • Email address (required to register with Let's Encrypt)

Getting Started

1. Deploy Acmebot

Azure (Public) Azure China Azure Government

2. Add application settings

Update the following configuration settings of the Function App:

  • Acmebot:Webhook

It has the same functionality as other Acmebot products, but supports Container Apps specific features. Azure DNS is currently required for use.

From here, I will actually use Acmebot to add a custom domain and certificate to the Container App.

Deploy Acmebot

Deploy Acmebot using the "Deploy to Azure" button in the README on GitHub.

After deployment is complete, add Azure AD authentication using App Service Authentication. Detailed instructions are provided in the README.

Setup RBAC (IAM) for Acmebot

Add the Contributor RBAC role to the deployed Acmebot for the resource group where Container Apps and Container Apps Environment are deployed.

Currently there is no RBAC role for Container Apps, so Contributor RBAC role must be assigned.

Since Acmebot requires Azure DNS for certificate issuance, assign the role of DNS Zone Contributor to Acmebot for Azure DNS as well.

Issue certificate

If the setup was successful, accessing the application will show a list of DNS Zones and Container Apps Environment.

The following simple screen allows you to select a DNS zone and issue a certificate for the required domain name.

Container Apps certificates are associated with the Container App Environment, so the Container Apps Environment must be explicitly specified when the certificate is issued.

If the certificate is successfully issued, it can be found in the list of certificates in the Container Apps Environment.

Acmebot is designed to attempt to automatically renew certificates associated with the Container Apps Environment.

Bind to Container App

An additional option is to add custom domain settings to any Container App at the same time the certificate is issued.

A custom domain is added to the Container App, but the actual A or CNAME DNS record is not automatically added and must be added manually. This is by design for safety.

After manually adding the A record, the browser will be able to view the Container App hosted in the HTTPS-protected Zone apex domain, as shown below.

In this way, Acmebot greatly reduces the effort of issuing certificates for Container Apps and automates their management.

Renew certificates

The default setting is to automatically renew certificates 30 days before they expire.

Upon successful renewal of the certificate by Acmebot, Container Apps will automatically use the new certificate.

Related post

If you are interested in the App Service and Key Vault version of Acmebot, please refer to the following post.

Enjoy Azure Container Apps and Let's Encrypt!
Items.Author: shibayan

Items.Title: 用python 4编程：条件语句 | Programming with python 4:Conditional statement
Items.Description:
迈克尔

发表于我们大多数人都熟悉现实生活中的条件陈述，例如“如果我有足够的钱，我会买一个球”，它给出的条件是，如果你有足够的钱，你会购买该物品，但如果没有，你就不会。 像所有编程语言一样，Python 也有一种编写条件语句的方法，使用“if…..”语句 示例是代码


运行时


当它小于金额但如果它更大时，程序将不会做任何事情：

如果满足条件，则执行该操作，否则将执行该操作。 此外，在 if 语句中，您可能希望程序在条件不满足时运行，您可以使用“else”语句。 运行时


或者

因此，如果钱大于 500，它会发送一条消息说买了球，但如果不是，它会发送一条消息说没有足够的资金。

希望你喜欢这个简短的课程，更多关于这方面的内容将在下周上传，阅读，喜欢，评论

亚历克斯马尔多纳多索托 - May 3 5月3日

Python, like all programming languages, also has a way of writing conditional statements like this, with the use of "if….." Statement An example is a code

The program won't do anything when it's less than the amount but if it's greater:

If the condition is fulfilled the operation is carried out, but if not it would be carried out. In addition, in the if statement it's possible you want the program to run when the condition is not fulfilled here you use the "else" statement. The else is designed so if the condition under the if statement isn't fulfilled it will be Example

Items.Title: 使用 HTML、CSS 和可排序 JS 拖放或排序列表 | Drag & Drop or Sortable List using HTML, CSS, and Sortable JS
Items.Description:
朋友们好，今天在这个博客中，您将学习如何使用 HTML、CSS 和 Sortable JS 创建拖放或可排序列表。 在我们之前的博客中，我们看到了如何在 Javascript 中创建拖放或浏览功能。 之前分享了很多javascript相关的项目，大家可以去看看，别忘了去看看HTML、CSS、Javascript项目。

拖放是一种标记设备手势，其中用户通过“抓取”虚拟物并将其拖动到单独的区域或另一个虚拟物上来选择它。 你可能会喜欢这些：

在这个程序[Drag & Drop List or Sortable List]中，网页上有五个列表或卡片，它们是可拖动的项目或列表。 用户可以轻松地重新排序未排序列表中的项目，为用户提供特定操作和修改的视觉维度。 如果你觉得难以理解我在说什么。 您可以查看源代码或预览。

预览可在此处获得。

HTML Code HTML 代码

<!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en"> <!-- --------------------- Created By InCoder --------------------- --> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> <title>Drag & drop or sortable List - InCoder</title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="main.css"> <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.1.1/css/all.min.css"> </head> <body> <div class="container"> <div class="title"> <h3>Drag & drop or sortable List</h3> </div> <div class="items"> <div class="item"> <p>Dragable Item 1</p> <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span> </div> <div class="item"> <p>Dragable Item 2</p> <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span> </div> <div class="item"> <p>Dragable Item 3</p> <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span> </div> <div class="item"> <p>Dragable Item 4</p> <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span> </div> <div class="item"> <p>Dragable Item 5</p> <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span> </div> <div class="item"> <p>Dragable Item 6</p> <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span> </div> </div> </div> <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/Sortable/1.15.0/Sortable.min.js"></script> <script> let items = document.querySelector('.items') new Sortable(items, { animation: 200, handle: '.moveHand', ghostClass: 'drag' }); </script> </body> </html>

CSS Code CSS 代码

/* --------------------- Created By InCoder --------------------- */ @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Poppins:ital,wght@0,100;0,200;0,300;0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;0,800;0,900;1,100;1,200;1,300;1,400;1,500;1,600;1,700;1,800;1,900&display=swap'); *{ margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif; } body{ display: flex; height: 100vh; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background-color: #1c015e; } .container{ padding: .5rem 1rem; border-radius: .5rem; background-color: #fff; width: clamp(20rem, 4vw, 25rem); } .container .title { text-align: center; margin-bottom: .5rem; } .container .item { display: flex; color: #fff; margin: .3rem 0; align-items: center; border-radius: .3rem; padding: .5rem .5rem; background-color: #1c015e; justify-content: space-between; } .container .item span { width: 2rem; height: 2rem; cursor: move; display: grid; place-items: center; } .container .item span:hover, .container .item.drag { background-color: #2e0e7c; }



原文:

Hello friends, today in this blog, you will learn how to create drag & drop or sortable lists using HTML, CSS, and Sortable JS. In our previous blog, we saw how to create drag & drop or browse features in Javascript. Earlier, I shared many projects related to javascript, you can check if you want, and don't forget to check HTML, CSS, and Javascript projects.

Drag and drop is a marking device gesture in which the user selects a virtual thing by “grabbing” it and dragging it to a separate area or onto another virtual thing. Sortable JS is a Javascript library that lets you sort or reorder lists by dragging and dropping list items.

You may like these:

In this program [Drag & Drop List or Sortable List], there are five lists or cards on the webpage and these are draggable items or lists. Users can easily reorder the items in an underorder list, giving users a visual dimension to particular actions and modifications. If you’re feeling difficulty understanding what I am saying. You can check the source code or preview.

The preview is available here.

HTML Code 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<!-- --------------------- Created By InCoder --------------------- -->
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Drag & drop or sortable List - InCoder</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="main.css">
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.1.1/css/all.min.css">
</head>

<body>
    <div class="container">
        <div class="title">
            <h3>Drag & drop or sortable List</h3>
        </div>
        <div class="items">
            <div class="item">
                <p>Dragable Item 1</p>
                <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span>
            </div>
            <div class="item">
                <p>Dragable Item 2</p>
                <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span>
            </div>
            <div class="item">
                <p>Dragable Item 3</p>
                <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span>
            </div>
            <div class="item">
                <p>Dragable Item 4</p>
                <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span>
            </div>
            <div class="item">
                <p>Dragable Item 5</p>
                <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span>
            </div>
            <div class="item">
                <p>Dragable Item 6</p>
                <span class="moveHand"><i class="fa-solid fa-grip-lines"></i></span>
            </div>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/Sortable/1.15.0/Sortable.min.js"></script>

    <script>
        let items = document.querySelector('.items')

        new Sortable(items, {
            animation: 200,
            handle: '.moveHand',
            ghostClass: 'drag'
        });
    </script>
</body>

</html>

CSS Code 

/* --------------------- Created By InCoder --------------------- */

@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Poppins:ital,wght@0,100;0,200;0,300;0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;0,800;0,900;1,100;1,200;1,300;1,400;1,500;1,600;1,700;1,800;1,900&display=swap');

*{
    margin: 0;
    padding: 0;

}

body{
    display: flex;
    height: 100vh;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    background-color: #1c015e;
}

.container{
    padding: .5rem 1rem;
    border-radius: .5rem;
    background-color: #fff;
    width: clamp(20rem, 4vw, 25rem);
}

.container .title {
    text-align: center;
    margin-bottom: .5rem;
}

.container .item {
    display: flex;
    color: #fff;
    margin: .3rem 0;
    align-items: center;
    border-radius: .3rem;

    justify-content: space-between;
}

.container .item span {
    width: 2rem;
    height: 2rem;
    cursor: move;
    display: grid;
    place-items: center;
}

.container .item span:hover,
.container .item.drag {
    background-color: #2e0e7c;
}
